domain: Mbuti/Twa Pygmy hocket polyphony (Congo Ituri Forest hunter-gatherers; rapid alternation of single notes between singers — each singer contributes one note at a time; yodel chest/head register alternation; communal egalitarian coordination)
specific_mechanism: K-draft phase-offset interlocking speculative decoding: K small drafter models D_1..D_K each emit ONE token per step in modulo-K phase-offset pattern (drafter i emits token at output position p where p mod K = i). Single target model T verifies the K-token batch cohesively; on rejection at position p the affected drafter's slot remains untouched and is re-emitted on next pass.
llm_application: MBUTI-HOCKET-DECODE: a K-draft phase-offset interlocking speculative decoder. (1) K small drafter models D_1..D_K each fine-tuned/distilled to produce ONE token at a time; (2) drafter i emits token only at output positions p where p mod K = i (strict modulo-K assignment, like hocket single-note-per-singer); (3) all K drafted tokens forming positions p..p+K-1 are verified COHESIVELY by single target model T in one forward pass; (4) on partial rejection, accepted prefix kept and rejected positions re-drafted on next pass. Differs from P-EAGLE parallel speculative (single drafter for multiple tokens), Parallel Token Prediction 2512.21323 (parallel-mask interleaving for diffusion-style, not K-drafter modulo-K), Multi-Token Prediction 2507.11851 (target predicts multi-token natively, no drafter swap), Judge Decoding (judge-based draft verification single-drafter). Differs from R415 BOWL-PARTIAL (K decoders at strategy-space golden-angle offsets sampling DIVERSE candidates, judge-select-best) by STRICT MODULO-K phase-offset SINGLE-TOKEN position assignment + cohesive verification.
novelty_claim: P-EAGLE + Parallel Token Prediction + Multi-Token Prediction + Judge Decoding cover parallel speculative decoding space. MBUTI-HOCKET-DECODE adds (a) K drafters + (b) strict modulo-K SINGLE-TOKEN position assignment + (c) cohesive verification on K-token batch. Recombination.
